checklist.notes: get_oauth_url() method correctly builds OAuth URL with client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope, state, access_type=offline, and prompt=consent. URL construction is proper using query string parameters.

checklist.item: State parameter present for CSRF protection
checklist.notes: State parameter is generated using secrets.token_urlsafe(32) for cryptographic randomness. Stored in database (oauth_states table) with 10-minute expiration. State is validated in callback using validate_oauth_state() which checks expiration and marks as used (single-use).

exchange_code_for_token() method properly exchanges authorization code for access token via Google's token endpoint. Includes all required parameters: code, client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, grant_type. Uses httpx AsyncClient with 30s timeout and proper error handling.

create_access_token() generates JWT with comprehensive payload: sub (user_id), email, role, exp, iat, jti. Uses configurable JWT_SECRET, JWT_ALGORITHM (HS256), and JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS. Token includes unique JTI for potential revocation tracking.

verify_google_token() fetches user info from Google's userinfo endpoint, validates email_verified flag, and extracts email, name, picture, google_id. Properly checks verified_email before proceeding with authentication.

_is_allowed_domain() enforces domain restriction by checking email.endswith('@your-domain.com'). Called during token verification before user creation. Database-level constraint also enforced via CheckConstraint in User model. Configuration uses ALLOWED_EMAIL_DOMAIN setting.

Refresh tokens are database-backed (refresh_tokens table) with hashed storage using SHA256. validate_refresh_token() checks hash, revocation status, and expiration. Implements token rotation via rotate_refresh_token() - revokes old token and creates new one (single-use pattern). Includes audit trail with user_agent and ip_address.

Multiple revocation methods implemented: revoke_refresh_token() for single token, revoke_all_user_tokens() for logout from all devices. Revocation sets is_revoked=True and revoked_at timestamp. Cleanup method cleanup_expired_tokens() removes expired tokens from database.

OAuth state is cryptographically secure (secrets.token_urlsafe(32)), stored in database with expiration (10 minutes), and marked as single-use after validation. State validation occurs before token exchange. Expired states are cleaned up via cleanup_expired_states().

Custom exceptions (AuthenticationError, AuthorizationError) with specific error codes. Comprehensive error handling in routes: domain restrictions (403), invalid tokens (401), expired tokens (401 with TOKEN_EXPIRED), state validation failures. Logging at appropriate levels (info for success, warning for failures, error for exceptions).

missing_test_coverage: OAuth state creation and validation (async DB operations)
Code exchange for token (async HTTP operations)
Integration tests for full OAuth flow
test_details.test_notes: Existing tests cover synchronous logic and JWT operations well. Missing tests for async database operations and HTTP interactions. Tests do not use async fixtures or mock database sessions.

issues.description: Test suite missing async database operations coverage
issues.details: test_auth_service.py only tests synchronous methods. Async methods (create_oauth_state, validate_oauth_state, exchange_code_for_token, verify_google_token, create_refresh_token, validate_refresh_token, rotate_refresh_token, revoke_refresh_token, revoke_all_user_tokens) are not tested.
issues.recommendation: Add async tests using pytest-asyncio and mock database sessions to test async methods. Use fixtures for AsyncSession mocking.
issues.impact: Medium - core async functionality not validated via automated tests

issues.description: No integration tests for OAuth flow
issues.details: Full OAuth flow (state generation -> OAuth redirect -> callback -> token exchange -> user creation -> token issuance) not tested end-to-end.
issues.recommendation: Add integration tests that mock Google OAuth endpoints and test complete flow including error scenarios (invalid state, expired state, domain mismatch, invalid Google token).
issues.impact: Medium - critical authentication flow not validated holistically

issues.description: Hardcoded datetime.utcnow() instead of timezone-aware datetime
issues.details: user_service.py uses datetime.utcnow() (naive datetime) instead of datetime.now(timezone.utc) which is timezone-aware. This is inconsistent with auth_service.py which correctly uses timezone.utc.
issues.recommendation: Replace datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc) for consistency and timezone awareness.
issues.impact: Low - functional but inconsistent, may cause issues with timestamp comparisons

issues.description: Missing docstring examples for complex methods
issues.details: Methods like get_oauth_url, exchange_code_for_token, verify_google_token could benefit from usage examples in docstrings.
issues.recommendation: Add docstring examples showing typical usage patterns, especially for OAuth flow methods.
issues.impact: Low - code is clear but examples would improve developer experience

recommendations.details: Add pytest-asyncio tests with mocked AsyncSession for all async auth_service methods. Include fixtures for User model, RefreshToken model, OAuthState model. Test success and failure paths.

recommendations.description: Add integration tests for OAuth flow
recommendations.details: Create integration tests using httpx.AsyncClient and database test fixtures. Mock Google OAuth endpoints using respx or similar. Test complete authentication lifecycle including edge cases.
Update user_service.py to use datetime.now(timezone.utc) instead of datetime.utcnow() for consistency with auth_service.py and database models.

recommendations.description: Add rate limiting to auth endpoints
recommendations.details: Consider adding rate limiting specifically to /auth/login and /auth/google endpoints to prevent brute force or enumeration attacks. Settings already include RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE but implementation not visible in reviewed files.
Track metrics for: successful logins, failed logins, token refreshes, token revocations, domain restriction violations. Useful for security monitoring and debugging.

strengths: Single-use OAuth state tokens with database storage and expiration
Refresh tokens hashed with SHA256 before database storage
Token rotation on refresh (single-use refresh tokens)
Domain restriction enforced at multiple levels (application + database constraint)
HTTP-only cookies for refresh tokens in production
Secure flag on cookies in production mode
Proper separation of access and refresh tokens
Revocation capabilities (single token + all user tokens)
considerations: No explicit PKCE implementation (not critical for server-side OAuth but would be defense-in-depth)
No visible rate limiting on auth endpoints (may be implemented elsewhere)
No explicit token blacklisting for JWTs (relies on short expiration time)
No visible account lockout mechanism after repeated failures

strengths: Clean separation of concerns (service layer, routes, middleware)
Proper use of SQLAlchemy async patterns
RESTful API design with proper status codes
notes: Database models well-structured with proper indexes
CSRF protection implemented correctly with OAuth state
Token rotation follows security best practices
Middleware provides flexible auth (required, optional, active-only)

compliance_notes.oauth2_compliance: Full OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow implemented correctly
compliance_notes.csrf_protection: State parameter properly implemented per OAuth 2.0 spec
compliance_notes.jwt_standards: JWT tokens follow RFC 7519 with standard claims (sub, exp, iat, jti)
compliance_notes.security_best_practices: Follows OWASP recommendations for token management and session handling

summary: The OAuth/Auth integration is production-ready with excellent security posture and clean architecture. All 10 checklist items pass validation. The implementation follows OAuth 2.0 specifications, implements CSRF protection correctly, uses database-backed refresh tokens with rotation, enforces domain restrictions, and provides comprehensive error handling. Main gap is test coverage for async operations and integration tests. Code quality is excellent with proper type hints, async patterns, and separation of concerns. Security implementation is strong with cryptographic randomness, token hashing, single-use patterns, and comprehensive audit trails.
next_steps: Add async test coverage for database operations
Implement integration tests for complete OAuth flow
Consider adding rate limiting and monitoring metrics
